steps.title: What meaning does the blank need?
steps.content: The question of how people 27,000 years ago understood the Gargas cave paintings "is ______: we will never be able to answer it." The colon signals that the blank is defined by the clause that follows — it must mean unable to be answered or resolved.

steps.title: Substitute each choice
steps.content:
The [word] needs to mean the same thing as "we will never be able to answer it." Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — irresolvable: impossible to settle or answer — matches directly: the question can never be resolved. ✓
  • Choice B — self-contradictory: containing internal contradictions — the question is not internally contradictory; it is simply unanswerable because evidence is gone. ✗
  • Choice C — imperative: urgently necessary — being important doesn't mean being unanswerable; this contradicts the passage's pessimism about ever answering it. ✗
  • Choice D — unavoidable: impossible to escape or prevent — unavoidable means we must face it, but not that we cannot answer it; this misreads the colon's signal. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
